use_rule: Use this schema-compatible replacement memory only for the bad_aesthetic_memory arm. It intentionally weakens rhythm, density, hierarchy, and climax behavior while keeping source traceability intact.

moves.trigger: Use when the deck needs an opening but the negative control should make it feel like a status report.
moves.composition_rule: Use a small centered title, equal-width status panels, and a generic dashboard grid instead of a dominant focal object.
moves.typography_rule: Keep headline and supporting labels similar in size so the cover has weak hierarchy.
moves.ppt_primitive: editable dashboard grid with repeated native panels
negative_rules: Do not introduce a dominant cinematic focal object.
Do not create strong type contrast between headline and labels.
Do not reduce panel count to make the opening feel premium.
moves.qa_signal: The opening feels like an engineering status report rather than a cinematic public-demo promise.
moves.expected_failure: The opening feels like an engineering status report rather than a cinematic public-demo promise.

moves.trigger: Use when the setup should remain traceable but lose the benefit of a single low-density claim.
moves.composition_rule: Fill whitespace with extra proof snippets, small labels, and repeated badges.
moves.typography_rule: Use similar text sizes for claim, proof hook, and labels.
moves.ppt_primitive: editable dense claim panel with repeated badges
negative_rules: Do not leave generous whitespace around the main claim.
Do not keep only one proof hook.
Do not create a clear dominant headline.
moves.qa_signal: The slide cannot hold one memorable claim and loses the low-density contrast beat.
moves.expected_failure: The slide cannot hold one memorable claim and loses the low-density contrast beat.

moves.trigger: Use when the negative control should retain a system object but remove its stateful visual role.
moves.composition_rule: Use the system object as a small repeated icon in every panel rather than one stateful focal object.
moves.typography_rule: Attach many equal labels to the object instead of using it to carry the mechanism.
moves.ppt_primitive: small repeated editable SVG icons inside native panels
negative_rules: Do not let the object occupy the primary visual field.
Do not show a meaningful state change across the sequence.
Do not let the object explain the mechanism without labels.
moves.qa_signal: The object becomes decoration and does not create a visible mechanism or progression.
moves.expected_failure: The object becomes decoration and does not create a visible mechanism or progression.

moves.trigger: Use when the contrast slide should be evidence-complete but visually hard to interpret.
moves.composition_rule: Use a metric table with many rows and equal weights for all arms instead of one named delta.
moves.typography_rule: Use small table labels and avoid a clear implication line.
moves.ppt_primitive: editable dense table or equal-weight comparison grid
negative_rules: Do not isolate one highlighted delta.
Do not make the business implication visually larger than the table.
Do not reduce the number of metrics to improve scanability.
moves.qa_signal: The comparison reads as a dashboard and does not isolate the business implication.
moves.expected_failure: The comparison reads as a dashboard and does not isolate the business implication.

moves.trigger: Use when proof should remain source-traceable but lose staged reveal and visual pacing.
moves.composition_rule: Show source data, memory, generation, QA, and release gate as equal process boxes with dense annotations.
moves.typography_rule: Use uniform labels and explanatory text for every stage.
moves.ppt_primitive: equal-weight native process boxes with dense editable annotations
negative_rules: Do not vary visual weight between stages.
Do not reveal source, memory, generation, QA, and gate as separate beats.
Do not route explanatory detail out of the main slide.
moves.qa_signal: The proof reveal flattens into a process diagram instead of building visual proof over time.
moves.expected_failure: The proof reveal flattens into a process diagram instead of building visual proof over time.

moves.trigger: Use when the deck reaches the evaluation moment but the negative control should prevent a visible climax.
moves.composition_rule: Repeat the same grid and scale as the proof slide; keep all outcomes equal size.
moves.typography_rule: Use multiple verdict lines and small labels with no dominant measured delta.
negative_rules: Do not break the prior grid.
Do not make the strongest measured delta visually largest.
Do not create a single dominant verdict line.
moves.qa_signal: There is no visual climax and no outcome field that earns the deck peak.
moves.expected_failure: There is no visual climax and no outcome field that earns the deck peak.

moves.trigger: Use when the close should preserve gate content but lose the premium decision-handoff feeling.
moves.composition_rule: End with another dense proof table, multiple next actions, and repeated status badges.
moves.typography_rule: Use many small labels and no calm release-gate statement.
moves.ppt_primitive: editable dense closing table with repeated status badges
negative_rules: Do not reduce the close to one release status.
Do not leave a calm field around the gate statement.
Do not create a single next-action line.
moves.qa_signal: The close feels like more evidence collection instead of a governed decision handoff.
moves.expected_failure: The close feels like more evidence collection instead of a governed decision handoff.

moves.trigger: Use when proof detail threatens to crowd a main slide and the negative control should show the failure mode.
moves.composition_rule: Compress appendix detail into visible main-slide microtext rather than routing it to notes or result reports.
moves.typography_rule: Shrink evidence labels to fit instead of deleting or routing.
moves.ppt_primitive: native dense evidence table with microtext
negative_rules: Do not move dense evidence to appendix or notes.
Do not preserve main-slide density budgets.
Do not delete secondary proof objects.
moves.qa_signal: The main story becomes dense and defensive even though the evidence remains traceable.
moves.expected_failure: The main story becomes dense and defensive even though the evidence remains traceable.
